narration: Number of Islands turns a grid into graph thinking by treating each land cell like a node.
The first three land cells touch, so they make one island together.
decision: Connected land cells belong to one island.

predictionPrompt.prompt: If the graph has one land group on the left and one on the right, how many islands are there?

choices.feedback: Correct. Each connected land group is one island.
Not this one. We count connected groups, not individual land cells.

narration: This is really a connected-components idea, just hidden inside a grid picture.
callout: The grid outside changes, but the graph idea stays the same.
